author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
On the words of the Gospel, Luke xi. 39, ‘Now do ye Pharisees cleanse the outside of the cup and the platter,’ etc.

ccel.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

title: On the words of the Gospel, Luke xi. 39, ‘Now do ye Pharisees cleanse the outside of the cup and the platter,’ etc.

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Ye have heard the holy Gospel, how the Lord Jesus in that which He said to the Pharisees, conveyed doubtless a lesson to His own disciples, that they should not think that righteousness consists in the cleansing of the body.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For every day did the Pharisees wash themselves in water before they dined; as if a daily washing could be a cleansing of the heart.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Then He showed what sort of persons they were.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He told them who saw them; for He saw not their faces only but their inward parts.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For that ye may know this, that Pharisee, to whom Christ made answer, thought within himself, he uttered nothing aloud, yet the Lord heard him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For within himself he blamed the Lord Christ, because He had so come to his feast without having washed.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He was thinking, the Lord heard, therefore He answered.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What then did He answer?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Now do ye Pharisees wash the outside of the platter; but within ye are full of guile and ravening.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What! is this to come to a feast! how did He not spare the man by whom He had been invited?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Yea rather by rebuking He did spare him, that being reformed He might spare him in the judgment.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And what is it that He showeth to us?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: That Baptism also which is conferred once for all, cleanses by faith.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now faith is within, not without.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Wherefore it is said and read in the Acts of the Apostles, “Cleansing their hearts by faith.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And the Apostle Peter thus speaks in his Epistle; “So too hath He given you a similitude from Noah’s ark, how that eight souls were saved by water.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And then he added, “So also in a like figure will baptism save us, not the putting away of the filth of the flesh, but the answer of a good conscience.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “This answer of a good conscience” did the Pharisees despise, and washed “that which was without;” within they continued full of pollution.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And what did He say to them after this?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “But rather give alms, and behold all things are clean unto you.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: See the praise of alms, do, and prove it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But mark awhile; this was said to the Pharisees.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: These Pharisees were Jews, the choice men as it were of the Jews.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For those of most consideration and learning were then called Pharisees.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They had not been washed by Christ’s Baptism; they had not yet believed on Christ, the Only-begotten Son of God, who walked among them, yet was not acknowledged by them.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How then doth He say to them, “Give alms, and behold all things are clean unto you”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If the Pharisees had paid heed to Him, and given alms, at once according to His word “all things would have been clean to them;” what need then was there for them to believe on Him?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But if they could not be cleansed, except by believing on Him, who “cleanseth the heart by faith;” what means, “Give alms, and behold all things are clean unto you”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us carefully consider this, and peradventure He Himself explains it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When He had spoken thus, doubtless they thought that they did give alms.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And how did they give them?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They tithed all they had, they took away a tenth of all their produce, and gave it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is no easy matter to find a Christian who doth as much.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: See what the Jews did.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Not wheat only, but wine, and oil; nor this only, but even the most trifling things, cummin, rue, mint, and anise, in obedience to God’s precept, they tithed all; put aside, that is, a tenth part, and gave alms of it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I suppose then that they recalled this to mind, and thought that the Lord Christ was speaking to no purpose, as if to those who did not give alms; whereas they knew their own doings, how that they tithed, and gave alms of the minutest and most trifling of their produce.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They mocked Him within themselves as He spake thus, as if to men who did not give alms.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Lord knowing this, immediately subjoined, “But woe unto you, Scribes and Pharisees, who tithe mint, and cummin, and rue, and all herbs.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: That ye may know, I am aware of your alms.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Doubtless these tithes are your alms; yea even the minutest and most trifling of your fruits do ye tithe; “Yet ye leave the weightier matters of the law, judgment and charity.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Ye have “left judgment and charity,” and ye tithe herbs.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This is not to do alms.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “These,” saith He, “ought ye to do, and not to leave the other undone.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Judgment and charity, justice and mercy;” and “not to leave the other undone.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Do these; but give the preference to the others.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If this be so, why did He say to them, “Do alms, and behold all things are clean unto you”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is, “Do alms”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is, “Do mercy”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If thou understand, begin with thine own self.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For how shouldest thou be merciful to another, if thou art cruel to thyself?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Give alms, and all things are clean unto you.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Do true alms.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is alms?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hear the Scripture; “Have mercy on thine own soul, pleasing God.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Do alms, “Have mercy on thine own soul, pleasing God.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thine own soul is a beggar before thee, return to thy conscience.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Whosoever thou art, who art living in wickedness or unbelief, return to thy conscience; and there thou findest thy soul in beggary, thou findest it needy, thou findest it poor, thou findest it in sorrow, nay perhaps thou dost not find it in need, but dumb through its neediness.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For if it beg, it “hungereth after righteousness.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now when thou findest thy soul in such a state (all this is within, in thy heart), first do alms, give it bread.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If the Pharisee had asked this question, the Lord would have said to him, “Give alms to thine own soul.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For this He did say to him; but he did not understand it, when He enumerated to them the alms which they were used to do, and which they thought were unknown to Christ; and He saith to them, “I know that ye do this, ‘ye tithe mint and anise, cummin and rue;’ but I am speaking of other alms; ye despise ‘judgment and charity.’

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In judgment and charity give alms to thine own soul.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is “in judgment”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Look back, and discover thyself; mislike thyself, pronounce a judgment against thyself.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And what is charity?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Love the Lord God with all thy heart, and with all thy soul, and with all thy mind; love thy neighbour as thyself:” and thou hast done alms first to thine own soul, within thy conscience.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Whereas if thou neglect this alms, give what thou wilt, give how much thou wilt; reserve of thy goods not a tenth, but a half; give nine parts, and leave but one for thine own self: thou doest nothing, when thou doest not alms to thine own soul, and art poor in thyself.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let thy soul have its food, that it perish not by famine.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Give her bread.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What bread, thou wilt say?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He speaketh with thee Himself.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If thou wouldest hear, and understand, and believe the Lord, He would say to thee Himself, “I am the Living Bread which came down from heaven.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Wouldest thou not first give this Bread to thine own soul, and do alms unto it?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If then thou believest, thou oughtest so to do, that thou mayest first feed thine own soul.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Believe in Christ, and the things which are within shall be cleansed; and what is without shall be clean also.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Let us turn to the Lord,” etc.
